title: German Sauerbraten Casserole
description: A hearty, comforting German casserole featuring tender beef, pickles, and a tangy sauce. Serve as a solo dinner or alongside your favorite sides.

ingredients.name: pickle juice
ingredients.notes: from a jar of dill pickles

ingredients.name: dill pickle slices
ingredients.notes: from a jar of dill pickles

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, brown sugar, black pepper, and salt. Add the beef cubes and toss to coat.
In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat. Brown the beef cubes on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the browned beef from the pot and set aside. Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook until it's softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the browned beef, pickle juice, apple cider vinegar, and 1 cup of water to the pot. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven. Braise the beef for 2 1/2 hours, or until it's tender.
Stir in the full-fat sour cream and cook for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Stir in the dill pickle slices and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Transfer the casserole to a serving dish and garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Serve the casserole hot, garnished with additional pickle slices and parsley if desired.
